title: Vanilla marshmallows
subtitle: Soft, pillowy marshmallows set with gelatine and coated in icing sugar
excerpt: A classic gelatin-based marshmallow: a sugar syrup beaten into bloomed gelatine until thick and cloud-like, set in a tin, and cut into squares coated in icing sugar and cornflour. Makes about 36.
sourceNotes: Marshmallows, confections made from sugar syrup whipped with gelatine, are documented in French confectionery from the mid-nineteenth century, originally made from the root of the marsh mallow plant (Althaea officinalis) as a thickening agent. The modern gelatin-based form is documented in American and British confectionery guides from the early twentieth century. The technique of streaming hot sugar syrup into bloomed gelatine and whipping to a thick foam is documented in twentieth-century household confectionery guides.

content.text: Marshmallows need a stand mixer: the mixture must be whipped for 8 to 10 minutes while it cools, and no hand mixer can sustain that. Oil the tin and dust it generously, and oil the knife before cutting. The coating of {{icing-sugar}} and {{cornflour}} keeps the marshmallows from sticking together.
is the first step and the one most often skipped, don't.
Bloom the gelatine and cook the syrup

Submerge the {{gelatine-leaves}} in a large bowl of cold water and leave for 5 minutes to bloom. Meanwhile, combine the {{caster-sugar}}, {{glucose-syrup}}, and 100 ml cold water in a saucepan. Heat, without stirring, until it reaches 127°C on a {{sugar-thermometer}}. While the syrup cooks, squeeze the excess water from the bloomed gelatine and place in the bowl of a stand mixer. Pour 50 ml boiling water over and stir until the gelatine dissolves completely.

With the mixer running on high, slowly pour the hot syrup down the side of the bowl into the gelatine. Add the {{vanilla-extract}}. Continue whipping on high for 8 to 10 minutes until the mixture is very thick, white, and tripled in volume. Mix the {{icing-sugar}} and {{cornflour}} together and dust a generously oiled 20 × 20 cm tin. Pour in the marshmallow mixture and smooth the surface. Dust the top with more icing sugar mixture. Leave to set at room temperature for at least 4 hours. Dust a board and knife with the remaining sugar mixture and cut into squares. Toss in the coating and shake off the excess.
